user_message: How should I coach Raj Mehta on Tailspin Toys? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 12:12 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 16:37 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 14:26 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Tailspin Toys

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into competitive positioning.

[00:08:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:11:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:14:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:18:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:22:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
